LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THOMAS DEAN'S MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT AND DISMISS CASE WITH PREJUDICE (CCP 583.210) MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT - THE MOTION TO VACATE THE JUDGMENT AGAINST MR. DEAN IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OBJECT TO THAT RELIEF. DEFENDANT DEAN'S MOTION TO DISMISS THIS ACTION BASED ON CCP 583.210 IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF OBTAINED JUDGMENT AGAINST MR. DEAN BASED ON CCP 187. UNDER THAT PROCEDURE, OAKLAND OPERA THEATER AND MR. DEAN ARE ALTER EGOS AND THE COURT WAS MERELY INSERTING THE CORRECT NAME OF THE REAL DEFENDANT. SEE MCCLELLAN V. NORTHRIDGE PARK TOWNHOME OWNERS ASS'N, (2001) 89 CAL.APP.4TH 746, 752. THE CURRENT RECORD DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF NEEDED TO SERVE A NEW SUMMONS ON MR. DEAN. THE ISSUES REGARDING WHETHER PLAINTIFF PROPERLY SERVED OAKLAND OPERA THEATER AND WHETHER THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS OR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS BARS THIS ACTION ARE NOT PROPERLY BEFORE THE COURT AT THIS TIME. THE COURT DENIES MR. DEAN'S FEE REQUEST PER CCP 128.5. THAT STATUTE DOES NOT APPLY TO THIS ACTION BECAUSE IT WAS FILED AFTER DECEMBER 31, 1994. SEE CCP 128.5(B)(1). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-27-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John F. Lee, Esq. of the Law Office of John F. Lee, Tel. 415.863.4336, for defendant and moving party;
Douglas B. Provencher, Esq. of Provencher & Flatt LLP, Tel. 707.284.2380, for plaintiff and opposing party by court call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT THOMAS DEAN's MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT and dismiss case with prejudice (CCP 583.210) MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT. The motion to vacate the judgment against Mr. Dean is granted as unopposed. Plaintiff does not object to that relief. Defendant Dean's motion to dismiss this action based on CCP 583.210 is denied. Plaintiff obtained judgment against Mr. Dean based on CCP 187. Under that procedure, Oakland Opera Theater and Mr. Dean are alter egos and the Court was merely inserting the correct name of the real defendant. See McClellan v. Northridge Park Townhome Owners Ass'n, (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 746, 752. The current record does not demonstrate that plaintiff needed to serve a new summons on Mr. Dean. The issues regarding whether plaintiff properly served Oakland Opera Theater and whether the statute of frauds or statute of limitations bars this action are not properly before the Court at this time. The Court denies Mr. Dean's fee request per CCP 128.5. That statute does not apply to this action because it was filed after December 31, 1994. See CCP 128.5(b)(1). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
